data.title: Instacart expands Caper Carts features to track EBT SNAP eligible items
data.description:
• Maplebear (NASDAQ:CART), more commonly known as Instacart, has launched additional features for its AI-powered Caper Carts that give shoppers access to new ways to save and track items eligible for government food assistance benefits.
  • These updates on the Caper Carts smart screen offer real-time budgeting capabilities, and eligibility tracking for Electronic Benefits Transfer for Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program ((EBT SNAP)), along with loyalty program sign-up, improved product recommendations, and omnichannel savings.
  • Shoppers can also earn “Cart Cash” by clipping exclusive coupons and complete shopping milestones, such as making an in-store purchase three times in a month, redeeming Cart Cash directly at checkout.
  • With enhancements that include digital coupons and a personalized shopping experience, Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) has nearly tripled the number of stores with Caper Carts across 15 states.

data.title: Taylor Swift and AMC tie up again for promo film distribution ahead of her album release
data.description:
The release party film for Taylor Swift's upcoming album will be shown across 8,000 screens in 50 countries over three days as part of a promotional event.

The pop musician is collaborating with AMC Theatres Distribution and distributor Piece of Magic Entertainment to showcase her film "TAYLOR SWIFT | THE OFFICIAL RELEASE PARTY OF A SHOWGIRL" in Latin America, Europe, and Asia to promote her 12th studio album, "The Life of a Showgirl."

The musician and her team reportedly shelled out $15M for the second promotional project in which AMC is involved and bypassed the traditional Hollywood studio system.

AMC said in a statement Thursday it has struck an agreement with multiple movie theater operators, including ODEON Cinemas, Cineworld, Cinépolis, Cinemex, Cinemark, Vue Cinemas, UGC, CGV, Event Cinemas, HOYTS, Kinepolis, Pathé Cinema, Vue International, CineStar, Helios, Major Cineplex, Cineworld, Nordisk, and UCI, among many others.

The company said the film will be played in all 540 AMC locations in the U.S., will be shown in Cinemark (NYSE:CNK) and Regal chains, and will run in Cineplex in Canada. AMC has also partnered with Variance Films to bring the film to thousands of other movie theater screens throughout North America.

The musician's 89-minute film will run in cinemas from October 3 to October 5 only. Her 2023 collaboration with AMC for the "TAYLOR SWIFT | THE ERAS TOUR" concert film managed to rake in more than $261M at the global box office.

Swift on Wednesday also announced that Target (TGT) will be the exclusive retailer for a limited, one-time-only pressing of the vinyl version of her album.

data.title: BOS secures $590,000 robotics follow-on order from Australian client
data.description:
• BOS Better Online Solutions' (NASDAQ:BOSC) robotics division has secured a new $590,000 follow-on order from an Australian manufacturer.
  • BOSC +2.36% premarket to $4.78.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Morgan Stanley upgrades Slide Insurance, citing stock is cheap, earnings will be strong
data.description:
Slide Insurance Holdings (NASDAQ:SLDE) was upgraded at Morgan Stanley Research, citing that the stock is cheap and earnings will be strong.

Shares were +4.91% to $14.75 after the market opened on Thursday.

"While the stock was pressured in recent weeks and post 2Q25 earnings due to fears of an active hurricane season and intensifying Florida market competition, we view the stock as currently attractive based on valuation & expect durable earnings power for the rest of the year as the current hurricane season gets more seasoned," said analyst Bob Jian Huang.

"With relatively benign 3Q25e catastrophe losses, the company could see strong annualized ROE upwards of +40% for 2025e vs mid 20s-30s for other Floridan peers. For the longer term, the company should continue to see durable earnings power due to its unique underwriting technology," said the analyst in a research note.

Morgan Stanley raised its recommendation on Slide Insurance to Overweight from Equal-weight, and maintained the price target at $18.00.

The rating aligns with the average Wall Street analysts rating of Buy.

data.title: Webull rated new Buy at Rosenblatt on crypto trading, international upside
data.description:
Rosenblatt analyst Chris Brendler started coverage on digital retail trading platform Webull (NASDAQ:BULL) with a Buy rating in a recent note, pointing to its international expansion and as well as a friendlier U.S. crypto regulatory environment.

Last month, the company said it has restored crypto trading for all of its U.S. customers, some two years after suspending the service when it attempted to go public against a backdrop of regulatory scrutiny under the Biden administration.

"Crypto trading peaked at 20% of Webull's revenuesin the last cycle, and while it likely won't approach this level anytime soon, crypto is a key driver of our above-consensus estimates," Brendler wrote in a note to clients.

The analyst also took note of Webull's (NASDAQ:BULL) international expansion, with licenses already secured in 14 key regions, such as Hong Kong, Singapore and Japan.

"These markets are earlier in the retail participation curve than the US with less competition, especially in the active trader segment," he wrote.

"Although Webull's strong domestic share gains have overshadowed its international traction to date, we believe foreign markets represent Webull's most significant medium-term growth opportunity," the note said.

Rosenblatt's Buy rating compares with the SA Quant system rating of Sell, as well as the average SA analyst rate of Hold and the average Wall Street analyst rate of Strong Buy.

Meanwhile, BULL shares retreated 4.6% in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Silexion Therapeutics regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Silexion Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SLXN) announced on Thursday that it has received a notification indicating that the company regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement rule of $1.00 per share for continued listing.
  • Additionally, Nasdaq confirmed that Silexion regained compliance with the $2.5 million minimum shareholders' equity requirement. Accordingly, the company will continue to be listed on Nasdaq.
  • SLXN is -3.5% to $3.72.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Yields advance after strong economic data dents rate cut bets; US10Y reclaims 4.20%
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Thursday morning after strong economic data dented Federal Reserve rate cut expectations. Yields have generally been on an upswing amid an extended bond selloff since last week's decision by the central bank to cut interest rates for the first time in nearly nine months.

The Commerce Department revised second-quarter U.S. GDP growth higher to an annualized rate of 3.8% in its third estimate from 3.3%. Meanwhile, initial jobless claims data dropped for the second consecutive week. 

The two indicators pushed yields off their September lows, with the rate-sensitive U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) rising 5 basis points to 3.66%—its highest level since September 2. Meanwhile, the benchmark U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) added 4 basis points to 4.20%, the highest since September 4. 

See how yields trade across the curve here on Seeking Alpha’s bond page. 

“The U.S. economy is resilient and the strong GDP is another indication that we are not at risk of any kind of recession, even with slowing labor market growth,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated. 

Still, despite Thursday’s uptick, Treasury yields remain well below where they began the year. Year-to-date, the 2-year has dropped 60 basis points, while the 10-year has declined by 32 basis points.

Treasury ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), and (NYSEARCA:BIL).

Bond ETFs: (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).

data.title: Metalero Mining announces $300,000 private placement to advance Benson project
data.description:
• Metalero Mining (TSXV:MLO:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement for gross proceeds of up to $300,000 to fund the next phase of work on its flagship Benson project, located in the prolific Quesnel Trough of central British Columbia.
  • The offering will consist of up to 1.25 million flow-through units at $0.24 per FT unit.
  • Each FT Unit will consist of one flow-through common share and one flow-through common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one additional FT share at $0.40 for one year from the date of issuance.
  • The proceeds will be used to support the Fall 2025 exploration work at Benson, including further sampling and ground geophysics.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Q/C Technologies begins trading under new ticker “QCLS”
data.description:
• Q/C Technologies (NASDAQ:QCLS) has officially changed its ticker symbol from “TNFA” to “QCLS”, effective today.
  • The rebranding reflects a strategic shift into quantum-class computing.
  • Q/C now holds exclusive global rights to LightSolver’s laser-based processing unit. The company will apply this technology to quantum-class cryptocurrency applications.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Planet Labs to build factory in Berlin, doubling production of Pelican satellites
data.description:
Planet Labs (NYSE:PL) on Thursday announced plans to establish a new satellite production facility in Berlin to meet rising demand for Earth observation and intelligence solutions in Europe. The investment, valued at more than €10 million ($11.7 million), is expected to create up to 70 jobs and expand the company’s Berlin workforce to about 220.

The new site will manufacture next-generation Pelican satellites, a high-resolution imaging system designed for persistent monitoring. Planet (NYSE:PL) already produces spacecraft at its San Francisco headquarters, where its Pelican production line is fully operational. The Berlin plant will double overall production capacity of the fleet, executives said.

“Europe needs its own eyes, and they need it now,” Will Marshall, co-founder and chief executive of Planet Labs (PL), said in the announcement. He cited Europe’s urgent need for autonomous intelligence capabilities, adding that the facility will enable faster deployment of satellites to support security and stability across the region.

German officials welcomed the investment as a sign of confidence in the country’s space sector. Dorothee Bär, federal minister of research, technology and space, said in the announcement that Planet’s (PL) expansion “clearly shows Germany’s attractiveness and competitiveness” in a strategically vital industry. Katherina Reiche, federal minister for economic affairs and energy, called the decision “a vote of confidence in Germany as a high-tech and space-industry hub” that would create skilled jobs and strengthen supply chains.

Martin Polak, managing director of Planet Labs (PL), said Berlin has long served as the company’s European headquarters, supporting global satellite operations and driving business across the EMEA region.

The expansion follows strong growth for Planet (PL) in Europe, including a €240 million multi-year agreement with the German government and a separate contract with NATO. Both deals highlight what the company described as a global imperative for enhanced daily monitoring, warning systems, and responsive space capabilities.
